images.caption:

DragonForce emerged December 2023 with a logo, a manifesto, and a business model that would pull in 363 victims across 30+ countries.

This wasn't a hacker. This was a cartel.

images.topic: The Binary Signature — Ransom Note Decoded
images.hook: The ransom note as crime scene evidence — real technical artifacts under forensic light make cybersecurity viscerally real for every viewer.
images.caption:
The moment you see this note — it's already too late.

DragonForce's ransom notes carry a signature. ChaCha8 encryption. Cross-platform reach. A negotiation portal on the dark web. A deadline.

Behind every note: a BYOVD attack that blinded your EDR using truesight.sys before you even knew they were inside.

212.5% attack surge in one year. This is what industrialized ransomware looks like.

#RansomNote #DragonForce #CyberCrime #IncidentResponse #DFIR

images.caption:

images.caption:

images.hook: Showing a trusted security tool physically destroyed by malware code that exploits the operating system's own kernel trust model is the most alarming technical concept a security professional can see visualised.
images.caption:
Your EDR didn't fail. DragonForce weaponised your OS against it.

BYOVD — Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver.

DragonForce loads a legitimate, Microsoft-signed driver — truesight.sys or rentdrv2.sys — into the Windows kernel. Because it's signed, the OS trusts it completely.

Then they use it to terminate every EDR and AV process on the machine from kernel level.

Your security tools never see the attack coming. By the time ChaCha8 encryption starts, your defences are already dead.

MITRE T1562.001 — Impair Defenses.

This is why layered defence and kernel-level telemetry matter more than ever.

#BYOVD #EDR #DragonForce #MITREAttack #CyberDefense

images.caption:
Here is exactly how DragonForce kills a network — step by step — mapped to MITRE ATT&CK.

Step 1: Initial Access via vishing (T1566.004)
Step 2: Credential theft from helpdesk (T1078)
Step 3: VPN/RDP lateral movement (T1021.001)
Step 4: BYOVD — truesight.sys loads (T1068)
Step 5: EDR termination — rentdrv2.sys (T1562.001)
Step 6: Privilege escalation to SYSTEM (T1068)
Step 7: AD enumeration + LSASS dump (T1003.001)
Step 8: Data exfiltration pre-encryption (T1041)
Step 9: ChaCha8 encryption deployed (T1486)
Step 10: Ransom demand delivered (T1491.002)

Knowing the chain is the first step to breaking it.

None of these require a $10M budget.

Defend accordingly.

images.topic: The Dragon Is Already Inside The Walls
images.hook: A sweeping cinematic landscape revealing the dragon coiled within a corporate network — already past every defense — creates the visceral dread that motivates immediate action from C-suite to SOC.
images.caption:
You're looking for the breach.

You're checking perimeter logs. Reviewing firewall rules. Running external scans.

By the time you notice unusual network traffic — the data is already gone. T1041 ran quietly for 72 hours.

363 organizations learned this the hard way.

Don't be 364.
